title: ～つもりだ
titleEn: Expresses the speakers intention or plan
explanation: Dùng để diễn tả dự định hoặc ý định của người nói. Ở N1, cần chú ý sắc thái cũng có thể là “tự cho là” trong một số ngữ cảnh. Cấu trúc: Verb-dictionary form + つもりだ.
explanationEn: The ～つもりだ grammar point is used to express the speakers intention or plan to do something. It translates as intend to, plan to, or think that (I will). Its often used with the verb in its dictionary form
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: ～つもりで
titleEn: Expresses the speakers intention or plan; with the intention of, planning to
explanation: Dùng để nói làm một việc với ý định, tâm thế hoặc giả định như X. Có thể hiểu là “với ý định”, “coi như”. Cấu trúc: Verb-dictionary form + つもりで.
explanationEn: The ～つもりで grammar point is used to express the speakers intention or mindset when doing something. It can often be translated as with the intention of or with the mindset that
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: ～ではすまない
titleEn: Expresses that just doing something is not enough or will not suffice
structure: Verb-dictionary form + ではすまない / い-Adjective + ではすまない / な-Adjective + ではすまない / Noun + ではすまない
structureEn: Verb-dictionary form + ではすまない / い-Adjective + ではすまない / な-Adjective + ではすまない / Noun + ではすまない
explanation: Dùng để nói chỉ X thôi thì không đủ để giải quyết, kết thúc hoặc bù đắp vấn đề. Thường hàm ý cần hành động thêm hoặc phải chịu trách nhiệm. Cấu trúc: Verb-dictionary form + ではすまない / い-Adjective + ではすまない / な-Adjective + ではすまない / Noun + ではすまない.
explanationEn: The grammar point ～ではすまない is used when the speaker strongly feels that an action or situation alone is not sufficient or acceptable, implying that more serious measures are needed or that the consequences are more severe
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: ～とあって
titleEn: Expresses a special situation or event due to certain circumstances
structure: Noun + とあって / (Sometimes Verb + とあって, depending on context)
structureEn: Noun + とあって / (Sometimes Verb + とあって, depending on context)
explanation: Dùng để nêu một hoàn cảnh đặc biệt làm nguyên nhân cho tình trạng phía sau. Thường gặp trong tin tức, mô tả sự kiện. Cấu trúc: Noun + とあって / (Sometimes Verb + とあって, depending on context).
explanationEn: The ～とあって grammar point is used to indicate that, due to a special or noteworthy circumstance, a particular situation has occurred. It often translates as because (it is)... or given that... in English, emphasizing the uniqueness of the cause
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: ～とあれば
titleEn: Expresses a condition; if, when, since
structure: Verb-casual + とあれば / い-Adjective + とあれば / な-Adjective + だとあれば / Noun + だとあれば
structureEn: Verb-casual + とあれば / い-Adjective + とあれば / な-Adjective + だとあれば / Noun + だとあれば
explanation: Dùng để nói nếu đúng là hoàn cảnh X thì người nói sẵn sàng làm hoặc điều phía sau là đương nhiên. Cấu trúc: Verb-casual + とあれば / い-Adjective + とあれば / な-Adjective + だとあれば / Noun + だとあれば.
explanationEn: The ～とあれば grammar point is used to express a condition or premise in which a certain outcome or action naturally follows. It can be translated as if, when, or since, depending on context. It emphasizes that under this specific condition, a certain response is expected
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: ～といえども
titleEn: Even if it is said that; Even though; Although
structure: Noun + といえども / Verb-plain form + といえども / Adjective + といえども
structureEn: Noun + といえども / Verb-plain form + といえども / Adjective + といえども
explanation: Dùng để nói dù là X, dù được gọi là X, thì vẫn có điều ngoại lệ hoặc hạn chế. Sắc thái trang trọng hơn ても. Cấu trúc: Noun + といえども / Verb-plain form + といえども / Adjective + といえども.
explanationEn: The ～といえども grammar point is used to show a contrast or contradiction. It is similar to even though or although in English. It can be used with nouns, verbs, adjectives, etc., to emphasize that the reality is different from the assumption or label
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: ～といったらありはしない
titleEn: Express extreme exclamation; extremely, unbelievable, unsurpassed
structure: Noun + といったらありはしない / な-Adjective + といったらありはしない
structureEn: Noun + といったらありはしない / な-Adjective + といったらありはしない
explanation: Dùng để cảm thán mức độ cực kỳ mạnh của một trạng thái, thường là tiêu cực. Có thể hiểu là “không thể tả nổi”. Cấu trúc: Noun + といったらありはしない / な-Adjective + といったらありはしない.
explanationEn: The ～といったらありはしない grammar point is used to express an extreme degree of something, often surprise or admiration. It can be loosely translated as unbelievably... or incredibly
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: ～といったらありゃしない
titleEn: Emphasizes that something is to the extreme; you have no idea how..., its incredibly
structure: Verb-casual + といったらありゃしない / い-Adjective + といったらありゃしない / な-Adjective + だ + といったらありゃしない / Noun + だ + といったらありゃしない
structureEn: Verb-casual + といったらありゃしない / い-Adjective + といったらありゃしない / な-Adjective + だ + といったらありゃしない / Noun + だ + といったらありゃしない
explanation: Dùng trong văn nói để nhấn mạnh mức độ cực đoan: “khỏi phải nói là... đến mức nào”. Sắc thái thân mật hơn ありはしない. Cấu trúc: Verb-casual + といったらありゃしない / い-Adjective + といったらありゃしない / な-Adjective + だ + といったらありゃしない / Noun + だ + といったらありゃしない.
explanationEn: The ～といったらありゃしない grammar point is an idiomatic expression used to emphasize that something is extremely or incredibly a certain way—often beyond words. It can be loosely rendered as you wouldnt believe how... or its indescribably
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: ～といったらない
titleEn: Expresses the extreme of something; nothing can be more ... than this
structure: い-Adjective + といったらない / な-Adjective + だといったらない / Noun + だといったらない
structureEn: い-Adjective + といったらない / な-Adjective + だといったらない / Noun + だといったらない
explanation: Dùng để nhấn mạnh một cảm xúc hoặc trạng thái đạt mức rất cao, gần như không gì hơn được. Có thể dịch linh hoạt là “vô cùng”, “cực kỳ”. Cấu trúc: い-Adjective + といったらない / な-Adjective + だといったらない / Noun + だといったらない.
explanationEn: The ～といったらない grammar point is used to emphasize the high degree or intensity of an action or state. It can be translated as “nothing can be more ... than this” or “it’s extremely...”. It conveys that something is so intense or extreme that it cannot be adequately described
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: ～ときている
titleEn: Highlights a situation or condition that is serious/extreme, often with a negative nuance
structure: Verb-casual / い-Adjective / な-Adjective（＋だ） / Noun（＋だ） + ときている
structureEn: Verb-casual / い-Adjective / な-Adjective（＋だ） / Noun（＋だ） + ときている
explanation: Dùng để nêu một tình trạng đã ở mức đáng chú ý hoặc nghiêm trọng, thường kèm sắc thái không hài lòng. Có thể hiểu là “lại còn...”. Cấu trúc: Verb-casual / い-Adjective / な-Adjective（＋だ） / Noun（＋だ） + ときている.
explanationEn: The grammar point ～ときている is used to emphasize that some circumstance is particularly notable—often negatively. It implies, “Given that things are so , it’s no wonder that...” or “Because the situation is (too) , such-and-such follows.” It is relatively informal and more common in spoken language
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)
